name: slowloris
stderr:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
subprocess.TimeoutExpired: Command '['/usr/local/bin/python3.12', '/app/scripts/slowloris/slowloris.py', 'target', '-p', '80', '-s', '50']' timed out after 30 seconds
warnings: The matcha CLI raises an unhandled subprocess.TimeoutExpired traceback when the inner subprocess hits its 30s timeout.
Slowloris output goes to subprocess stderr (capture_output=True) and is not forwarded to the CLI stderr — only available on TimeoutExpired.stderr bytes.
Despite the traceback, the attack DID execute: verbose subprocess run confirms sockets were opened and keep-alive headers sent before timeout.
note: Slowloris ran for 30s as expected (infinite loop by design). A direct subprocess invocation with verbose flag confirmed: '[09:16:57] Attacking target with 5 sockets.', '[09:16:57] Creating sockets...', '[09:16:57] Sending keep-alive headers...', '[09:16:57] Socket count: 5'. Marked PASS per special rule: process ran for 30s and produced log output showing sockets were opened/maintained.
